name: Panettone (Italian Fruitcake)
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Mixed dried fruit, coarsely chopped
3 ounces Chopped candied or fresh fruit
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour and salt onto a sheet of wax paper; set aside.
2. In a small bowl, sprinkle yeast over warm water (see yeast package for temperature); add 1 teaspoon sugar and stir to dissolve. Let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
3. In an electric mixer, beat eggs with remaining sugar until frothy. Add margarine and beat well. Continue beating while adding orange peel and brandy extract.
4. Gradually add the yeast mixture, then gradually beat in sifted flour. Beat at high speed for 5 minutes, adding fruit alternately until thoroughly combined.
5. Cover the bowl with a clean damp towel or plastic wrap and let rise in a warm draft-free area until doubled in volume, about 30 minutes.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F. Spray a 3-cup fluted mold with nonstick cooking spray.
7. Punch dough down, then turn into prepared mold. Bake in the middle of the oven on the center rack for 10 minutes.
8. Reduce temperature to 325°F and bake until top is browned and cake begins to pull away from mold, about 30 minutes longer (cover with foil if browning too quickly).
9. Unmold onto a wire rack and let cool. Makes 4 servings.
